extra.message: User-supplied 'username' query parameter is interpolated directly into an SQL query string via f-string in the GET /select endpoint. The double-quote wrapping around {username} does not prevent injection — an attacker can close the quote and inject arbitrary SQL (e.g., username="; DROP TABLE users; --).

All fields from the User Pydantic model (name, username, password, address, email, contact) are interpolated directly into an SQL INSERT statement via f-string in the PUT /user endpoint. No sanitization is applied — an attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through any field.
The 'username' parameter in the DELETE /user endpoint is interpolated directly into an SQL DELETE statement via f-string, allowing SQL injection.

The 'user.address' field in the DELETE /user endpoint is interpolated directly into an SQL DELETE statement without even being wrapped in quotes (no string delimiters), making SQL injection trivially exploitable.

The POST /find endpoint accepts arbitrary JSON via request.json() and passes it directly to MontyDB's users.find(query). An attacker can inject MongoDB operators (e.g., {"$ne": ""}, {"$regex": ".*"}, {"$where": "1"}) to bypass filters or extract all user data, enabling NoSQL injection.

When run_sql_query returns multiple rows, the raw tuple data is returned including the password column (index 3). While the single-row code path (lines 155-160) omits passwords from the dict, the multi-row path at line 162 returns {"users": _data} with all columns including password hashes exposed to the client.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: All data endpoints (/select, /find, /user, /reset) lack any form of authentication or authorization. Any unauthenticated user can query, insert, delete, and reset the entire user database. There is no auth middleware, no token/API key check, and no session validation on any endpoint.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The run_sql_query function catches only KeyboardInterrupt but not other exceptions (line 164 shows the original 'except Exception' was commented out). SQL errors, constraint violations, and database exceptions propagate as unhandled exceptions to FastAPI, potentially leaking database schema details and internal error information to clients.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
